BASIC.name: FOR loop
BASIC.code:
10 FOR I = 1 TO 10
20   PRINT I
30 NEXT I

BASIC.name: WHILE loop
BASIC.code:
10 X = 0
20 WHILE X < 5
30   PRINT X
40   X = X + 1
50 WEND

BASIC.name: IF / THEN / ELSE
BASIC.code:
10 X = 5
20 IF X > 3 THEN PRINT "big" ELSE PRINT "small"

BASIC.name: INPUT statement
BASIC.code:
10 INPUT "Enter a number: "; N
20 PRINT "You entered"; N

BASIC.name: Array declaration
BASIC.code:
10 DIM A(10)
20 FOR I = 1 TO 10
30   A(I) = I * 2
40 NEXT I

BASIC.name: String functions
BASIC.code:
10 S$ = "Hello"
20 PRINT LEN(S$), LEFT$(S$,3), RIGHT$(S$,2), MID$(S$,2,3)

Logo.name: Procedure definition
Logo.code:
TO SQUARE :SIZE
  REPEAT 4 [ FORWARD :SIZE RIGHT 90 ]
END
SQUARE 100

Logo.name: Nested procedure
Logo.code:
TO FLOWER :SIZE
  REPEAT 36 [ SQUARE :SIZE RIGHT 10 ]
END
TO SQUARE :S
  REPEAT 4 [ FORWARD :S RIGHT 90 ]
END
FLOWER 50

Pascal.name: Hello World
FOR loop
WHILE loop
Procedure
Function
RECORD type

Prolog.name: Facts and query
Prolog.code:
parent(tom, bob).
parent(bob, ann).
ancestor(X, Y) :- parent(X, Y).
ancestor(X, Y) :- parent(X, Z), ancestor(Z, Y).
?- ancestor(tom, ann).

Prolog.name: Findall
Prolog.code:
color(red). color(green). color(blue).
?- findall(X, color(X), Colors).

Forth.name: Factorial
Forth.code:
: FACT  DUP 1 <= IF DROP 1 ELSE DUP 1 - RECURSE * THEN ;
5 FACT .

Forth.name: Stack operations
Forth.code:
10 20 30  \ Three items on stack
DUP .   \ Duplicate top
SWAP .  \ Swap top two
DROP    \ Remove top

Forth.name: DEFER / IS
Forth.code:
DEFER ACTION
: HELLO  ." Hello" CR ;
: GOODBYE  ." Goodbye" CR ;
' HELLO IS ACTION
ACTION
' GOODBYE IS ACTION
ACTION

C.name: FOR loop
Function
Array

PILOT.name: Accept and respond
PILOT.code:
T:What is your name?
A:*name
T:Hello, *name!
END

PILOT.name: Match example
PILOT.code:
T:Type YES or NO:
A:
M:YES
TY:You said yes!
MN:TN:You said no.
END

Haskell.name: Map and filter
Haskell.code:
main :: IO ()
main = do
  let nums = [1..10]
  print $ map (*2) $ filter even nums

Haskell.name: Recursive factorial
Haskell.code:
factorial :: Int -> Int
factorial 0 = 1
factorial n = n * factorial (n - 1)

main :: IO ()
main = print (factorial 10)

JavaScript.name: Arrow function
JavaScript.code:
const square = n => n * n;
console.log(square(7));

JavaScript.name: Array methods
Destructuring
JavaScript.code:
const [a, b, c] = [10, 20, 30];
console.log(a + b + c);

Lua.name: Function
Lua.code:
function square(n)
  return n * n
end
print(square(7))

Lua.name: Table (array)
Lua.code:
local t = {10, 20, 30, 40, 50}
for i, v in ipairs(t) do
  print(i, v)
end

Fortran.name: Hello World
Fortran.code:
PROGRAM hello
  IMPLICIT NONE
  PRINT *, 'Hello, World!'
END PROGRAM hello

Fortran.name: DO loop
Fortran.code:
PROGRAM counting
  IMPLICIT NONE
  INTEGER :: i
  DO i = 1, 10
    PRINT *, i
  END DO
END PROGRAM counting

Fortran.name: Function
Fortran.code:
PROGRAM funcs
  IMPLICIT NONE
  INTEGER :: square
  PRINT *, square(7)
CONTAINS
  INTEGER FUNCTION square(n)
    INTEGER, INTENT(IN) :: n
    square = n * n
  END FUNCTION square
END PROGRAM funcs

Brainfuck.name: Count to 10 (output as chars)
Brainfuck.code:
++++++++++ sets cell 0 to 10
[
  >+        increment cell 1
  <-        decrement cell 0
]
>. output cell 1 (ASCII 10 = newline)

COBOL.name: Hello World
COBOL.code:
IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
PROGRAM-ID. HELLO.
PROCEDURE DIVISION.
    DISPLAY 'Hello, World!'.
    STOP RUN.

COBOL.name: Arithmetic
COBOL.code:
IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
PROGRAM-ID. ARITH.
DATA DIVISION.
WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
  01 A PIC 9(4) VALUE 100.
  01 B PIC 9(4) VALUE 250.
  01 C PIC 9(4).
PROCEDURE DIVISION.
  COMPUTE C = A + B.
  DISPLAY C.
  STOP RUN.
